Annotation

Conclusion and Recommendations based on this report and previous findings known to us
The patient shows elevated serum creatinine, proteinuria, glucosuria, bilirubinuria, and abnormal urine appearance (cloudy, brown, ammoniacal), suggesting significant renal dysfunction, possibly with hepatic involvement. Elevated ALT and low alkaline phosphatase may indicate liver injury. Urine findings (low specific gravity, ketonuria) further support renal impairment. Prompt nephrology and hepatology evaluation is recommended.
